title: Decompose figures to find volume (unit cubes)
description: Find the volume of irregular 3D figures made up of unit cubes by dividing the figures into rectangular prisms and finding the volume of each part.
topic_path: Decompose figures to find volume (unit cubes)

assessment_items.question_content:
Unit cube:  
[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of the following figure?**    
[[☃ input-number 1]] cubic units

Front View | Back View
:-: | :-:
![The front view of a figure shows the top and front side. It is three blocks high. Each layer has different color blocks. The top layer has 8 green blocks: 2 rows and 4 columns. The middle layer has 14 blue blocks: 2 rows and 7 columns. The bottom layer has 14 red blocks: 2 rows and 7 columns.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/29e6e23ae2f6187a5e6ca9b61497a1239780ac92)|![The back view of the same figure shows the bottom and back side. It is three blocks high. Each layer has different colors. In the bottom layer there are 14 red blocks: 2 rows and 7 columns. The middle layer has 14 blue blocks: 2 rows and 7 columns. The top layer has 8 green blocks: 2 rows and 4 columns.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ab66e4e902803c2b4470775ad7e3b9b5115f2aa5)
hints:
We can separate the figure into $2$ rectangular prisms and find the volume of each.


[[☃ image 1]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1}= 4 \times 2 \times3= {24} \text{ cubic units}$

[[☃ image 2]]



$\text{Volume of prism 2}= 3 \times 2 \times 2 = {12} \text{ cubic units}$

Now, we need to add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure} = {24} + {12} = {36} \text{ cubic units}$
The volume of the figure is ${36}  \text{ cubic units}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Unit cube:

**What is the volume of the following figure?**    
[[☃ input-number 1]] cubic units

Front View | Back View
:-: | :-:
![The front view of a figure shows the top and front side. It is five blocks high. Each layer has different color blocks. The fifth (top) layer has 2 purple blocks: 2 rows and 1 column. The fourth layer has 2 pink blocks: 2 rows and 1 column. The third layer has 2 green blocks: 2 rows and 1 column. The second layer has 6 blue blocks: 2 rows and 3 columns. The first (bottom) layer has 6 red blocks: 2 rows and 3 columns.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/72f91c95fa8558d8afaea08efc3327eb7ca033be)	| ![The back view of the same figure shows the bottom and back side. It is five blocks high. Each layer has different colors. In the first (bottom) layer there are 6 red blocks: 2 rows and 3 columns. The second layer has 6 blue blocks: 2 rows and 3 columns. The third layer has 2 green blocks: 2 rows and 1 column. The fourth layer has 2 pink blocks: 2 rows and 1 column. The fifth (top) layer has 2 purple blocks: 2 rows and 1 column.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c6d8892aa315b53fb4e2ee0796e0d9113a54df16)
hints:
We can separate the figure into $2$ rectangular prisms and find the volume of each.

[[☃ image 1]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1}= 2 \times 2 \times2 = {8} \text{ cubic units}$

[[☃ image 2]]



$\text{Volume of prism 2}= 1 \times 2 \times 5 = {10} \text{ cubic units}$

Now, we need to add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure} = {8} + {10} = {18} \text{ cubic units}$
The volume of the figure is ${18}  \text{ cubic units}$.

 Front View | Back View
:-: | :-: 
![The front view shows a figure from the top and front side. The figure is two blocks high. Each layer has different color blocks. The top layer has 18 blue blocks: 4 rows and 3 columns, followed by additional 3 columns in the first (front) row and 3 columns in the second row. The bottom layer has 18 red blocks: 4 rows and 3 columns, followed by additional 3 columns in the first (front) row and 3 columns in the second row.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/824bacf888adeb5c4b432bf894e452d61f4dc34a) | ![The back view of the same figure that shows the bottom and back side. It is two blocks high. Each layer has different colors. In the bottom layer there are 12 red blocks: 4 rows and 3 columns, and additional 6 red blocks: 2 rows and 3 columns. The top layer has 12 blue blocks: 4 rows and 3 columns, and additional 6 blue blocks: 2 rows and 3 columns.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cc8646a16f5642ea9cd3e392e9322b3539a7d349)
hints:
We can separate the figure into $2$ rectangular prisms and find the volume of each.

[[☃ image 1]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1}= 3 \times 4 \times2 = {24} \text{ cubic units}$

[[☃ image 2]]



$\text{Volume of prism 2}= 3 \times 2\times 2 = {12} \text{ cubic units}$

Now, we need to add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure}  = {24} + {12} = {36} \text{ cubic units}$
The volume of the figure is ${36}  \text{ cubic units}$.

The second layer has 8 blue blocks: 3 rows and 2 columns, followed by 2 additional columns in the back row. The first (bottom) layer has 8 red blocks: 3 rows and 2 columns, followed by 2 additional columns in the back row.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/850c698ce4e1d7e0370e255eb183945fad403ee5) | ![The back view of the same figure shows the bottom and back side. The second layer has 4 blue blocks: 1 row and 4 columns, and additional 4 blue blocks: 2 rows and 2 columns. The fourth (top) layer has 4 pink blocks: 1 row and 4 columns.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7239b08025cd6d62750dd3ba20ca6066a1e3f8bf)
hints:
We can separate the figure into $2$ rectangular prisms and find the volume of each.

[[☃ image 1]]

Volume of a rectangular prism is equal to length times width times height $(V =l\times w\times h)$.
 
[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Volume of prism 1}= 2\times 2 \times3 = {12} \text{ cubic units}$

[[☃ image 2]]



$\text{Volume of prism 2}= 4 \times 1 \times 4 = {16} \text{ cubic units}$

Now, we need to add to find the volume of the entire figure.

$\text{Volume of entire figure} = {12} + {16} = {28} \text{ cubic units}$
The volume of the figure is ${28}  \text{ cubic units}$.
